# UC-22.56.23 · TSA-SD multi-modal threat-intelligence sharing through CISA AIS / E-ISAC / ST-ISAC + DHS bidirectional flow

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*The US government and industry groups share warnings about new cyber attacks. We make sure we receive those warnings without interruption and act on them.*

---

## Description

Surveys threat-intel feed freshness; flags stale AIS / ISAC subscriptions.

## Value

Active threat-intel feeds are an enforcement-evidenceable element of TSA SDs; stale feeds undermine the SOC and the inspector evidence narrative.

## Implementation

(1) Subscribe to CISA AIS + relevant ISACs. (2) Wire indicators into `threat_intel` index. (3) Schedule UC-22.56.23 hourly. (4) Build the `TSA Threat-Intel Health` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.56.23` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `tsa_sd_clause="TSA-SD-multi-threat-intel"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Pause AIS feed; UC-22.56.23 MUST flag `ais_feed=stale` within 1 hour.

### Negative scenario

Resume feed; UC-22.56.23 MUST NOT escalate.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk ES with threat-intel framework.
- TAXII subscriptions to CISA AIS + ISACs.

## Step 1 — Wire feeds

Configure TAXII clients to write to `threat_intel` index with source label set to `CISA_AIS`, `E_ISAC`, `ST_ISAC`, `A_ISAC`.

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.56.23: TSA Threat-Intel Health]
cron_schedule = */60 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.56.23,reg=TSA-Surface,clause=TSA-SD-multi-threat-intel
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Runbooks

**`feed_status=stale`** (owner: Threat-Intel Lead, severity P2):
1. Within 4 hours: re-establish feed.
2. Within 24 hours: investigate gap window for any missed advisories.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

- **TLS / proxy issues**: AIS TAXII may hit egress proxy issues; capture cert errors.
- **TAXII version drift**: AIS migrated from TAXII 1.x to 2.1; ensure clients are current.

**TSA-specific failure modes**:
- **Classified-channel separation**: CC must coordinate cleared analyst engagement on classified threats.
- **DHS DEFEND-style coordination**: TSA may direct cross-sector coordination during incidents.

## SPL

```spl
| metadata type=sources index=threat_intel
| eval source_normalized = case(
    match(source, "(?i)cisa"), "CISA_AIS",
    match(source, "(?i)e-?isac"), "E_ISAC",
    match(source, "(?i)st-?isac"), "ST_ISAC",
    match(source, "(?i)a-?isac"), "A_ISAC",
    1=1, "other")
| eval hours_since_event = round((now() - lastTime) / 3600, 1)
| eval feed_status = case(
    isnull(lastTime), "never_seen",
    hours_since_event > 4 AND source_normalized!="other", "stale",
    1=1, "healthy")
| where feed_status IN ("never_seen","stale")
| table source_normalized hours_since_event feed_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Per-feed freshness. (Panel 2) Indicator-volume trend. (Panel 3) Operationalised vs raw indicators. (Panel 4) ISAC engagement log.

## Known False Positives

1. **Burst-feed cadence**: A-ISAC may publish in bursts; tune `expected_silence_hours`.
2. **Closed-circuit feeds**: TLP:RED indicators may go to non-Splunk storage; track in side-channel.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `tsa_threat_intel_exception_lookup`.

## References

- [CISA AIS Program](https://www.cisa.gov/automated-indicator-sharing-ais)
- [E-ISAC](https://www.eisac.com/)
- [ST-ISAC](https://www.surfacetransportationisac.org/)
- [A-ISAC](https://www.a-isac.com/)
